config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan terhadap individu adalah dasar agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan membatasi konflik antara hak orang banyak dan hak perorangan, bukan dengan menempatkan individu selalu di bawah. Saya melihat ini penting karena tanpa batas yang jelas, nama "kepentingan masyarakat" bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that idea can too easily be used to excuse abuse of power. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability and social order, especially after seeing how disorder can hurt ordinary people, but that does not mean individual rights should be treated as secondary by default. A healthy society needs balance: sometimes the public interest must limit personal freedom, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable. If “society first” becomes a blanket rule, it can silence dissent and protect authority more than it protects the public.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Pengalaman di Indonesia membuat saya cukup peka bahwa alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, apalagi ketika warga tidak punya posisi tawar yang kuat terhadap negara atau kelompok mayoritas. Memang ada situasi tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, ketika hak individu bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan umum untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan সমাজ sebenarnya mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran bagi ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the claim that society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value social stability and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily "for the good of society" can become a shield for overreach and silence ordinary people. The point about narrow, justified limits is important: in real life, public health or safety may require restrictions, but those restrictions should never become a blank check to override basic freedoms. A society is stronger when it protects individuals from arbitrary power, not when it asks them to surrender their rights by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan dari mayoritas, pembungkaman kritik, atau keputusan yang merugikan kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan hak harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan kebiasaan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan bersama justru mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective stability, because people here often value order and the ability to solve problems efficiently, but I also know how dangerous it is when “the public good” becomes a vague excuse to silence criticism or ignore ordinary people’s dignity. Public health, safety, and emergency situations can justify limited restrictions, but those limits must be temporary, proportional, and accountable. A society is not truly healthy if it treats individual rights as disposable whenever authority claims a collective purpose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
